spec.goal: Make the plan skill contract state the plan-file slug style explicitly so agents do not have to infer it from the `<feature-name>` placeholder.
success_criteria: plan-writing/SKILL.md states that plan files use `docs/plans/YYYY-MM-DD_<feature-slug>.json`, with a single underscore separator after the date and a kebab-case feature slug
plan-execution/SKILL.md mirrors the same file naming guidance for consumers
README.md no longer leaves the slug style implicit
Focused plan-writing and plan-execution smoke coverage guards the new wording
constraints: Keep the change scoped to naming guidance and paired smoke coverage
Do not change the persisted `plan/1` schema or helper script behavior
Keep examples aligned with existing `docs/plans/*.json` filenames in this repo

tasks.title: Audit plan-file naming surfaces
tasks.objective: Inspect the current plan-writing, plan-execution, README, and existing plan files so the wording change matches the repo's actual filename convention.
outputs: Confirmed repo-local naming convention for plan files

tasks.title: Clarify naming guidance in skill docs
tasks.objective: Update plan-writing, plan-execution, and the repo README to say that the date is followed by one underscore and the feature slug itself is kebab-case.
outputs: Explicit plan-file naming guidance in all user-facing entrypoints

tasks.title: Harden focused smoke coverage
tasks.objective: Update the plan-writing and plan-execution smokes to assert the explicit slug guidance, then rerun the focused verification set.
outputs: Focused smoke assertions for the explicit plan-file naming rule

triggers: Clarifying the slug style requires changing helper-script behavior instead of only documentation and smoke coverage
Existing plan filenames in the repo are inconsistent enough that one naming rule would be misleading
Another shared contract surface outside the touched files must become authoritative for plan naming

evidence: 2026-03-20: Audited README.md, plan-writing/SKILL.md, plan-execution/SKILL.md, and existing docs/plans/*.json files; repo-local saved plans already use a single underscore after the date plus kebab-case feature slugs such as 2026-03-19_review-request-entrypoint.json.

2026-03-20: Updated plan-writing/SKILL.md, plan-execution/SKILL.md, and README.md to replace the ambiguous `<feature-name>` placeholder with `<feature-slug>` and to state the explicit one-underscore-plus-kebab-case naming rule.

2026-03-20: Hardened dev/plan-writing/run_smoke.py and dev/plan-execution/run_smoke.py to assert the explicit filename guidance; verified with python3 dev/plan-writing/run_smoke.py, python3 dev/plan-execution/run_smoke.py, and git diff --check.

local_checkpoint.contradictory_evidence: If another checked-in surface uses snake_case or a different separator for real plan files, tightening the wording to kebab-case would be wrong.
local_checkpoint.current_theory: The repo already uses kebab-case feature slugs in saved plan filenames; the ambiguity comes from `<feature-name>` placeholders that never state the slug style explicitly.
local_checkpoint.missing_evidence: Need to confirm all touched user-facing entrypoints and focused smokes can align on one explicit naming sentence.
local_checkpoint.next_action: Update plan-writing, plan-execution, README, and the focused smokes to encode the explicit underscore-plus-kebab-case rule.
